body:
'gittoc reject T-42' currently has no way to record why the ticket was rejected. Today users must run 'gittoc note T-42 "reason"' then 'gittoc reject T-42' — two commits, easy to skip the note. Add an optional reason argument wired into the rejected event's text field:

  gittoc reject T-42 -m "out of scope"
  gittoc reject T-42 --reason "duplicate of T-3"

The reason becomes the event_text for the 'rejected' event, visible in history/log without a separate note.
title: reject: accept a reason/message
